title: delegation — PHP attribute contracts
description: Two attributes for lifecycle listeners. `#[AsDelegationListener]` marks a class as a subscriber to role_delegations events (Created / Activated / Expired / RevokedEarly / SelfOverlapDetected). `#[AsImpersonationListener]` marks a class as a subscriber to impersonation_sessions events (Started / Ended / Extended / ForcedTerminate / AttemptRefused). Both are discovered at boot by `DelegationServiceProvider` so internal services under `notifications`, `observability`, `compliance`, and `workflow/approvals` react without hard-coded event bindings.

attributes.name: AsDelegationListener

params.name: events
params.description: Which delegation events this class reacts to. Wildcard `*` = every event under `Academorix\Delegation\Events\*` matching the delegation aggregate (Created / Activated / Expired / RevokedEarly / SelfOverlapDetected). Specific list e.g. `['DelegationRevokedEarly']` subscribes to that event only. Validated against the events map at boot; typos abort application boot.

params.name: queue
params.description: Queue name for asynchronous dispatch. Set to `null` for synchronous listeners (rare — reserve for audit tap-outs that MUST land in the same request cycle). Common values: `notifications` (default), `notifications-critical` (for DelegationSelfOverlapDetected — a security-sensitive signal that must not queue behind a backlog).

params.name: priority
params.description: Listener execution order. Lower values run first. Reserve 1–99 for framework-owned listeners (audit stamping, cache invalidation). Example: `InvalidateDelegationResolverCache` runs at priority=10 so the resolver cache is fresh before any downstream listener queries it.

attributes.name: AsImpersonationListener

params.name: events
params.description: Which impersonation events this class reacts to. Wildcard `*` = every event under `Academorix\Delegation\Events\*` matching the impersonation aggregate (Started / Ended / Extended / ForcedTerminate / AttemptRefused). Specific list e.g. `['ImpersonationSessionForcedTerminate']` subscribes to that event only.

params.name: queue
params.description: Queue name for asynchronous dispatch. DEFAULT `notifications-critical` because impersonation events have SLA implications — SOC 2 evidence writes cannot be delayed by a backlog. Override to `notifications` for lighter listeners (e.g. analytics tracking) where a moderate lag is acceptable.

params.name: priority
params.description: Listener execution order. Lower values run first. Reserve 1–99 for framework-owned listeners. Example: `WriteHeavyAuditRow` on ImpersonationSessionStarted runs at priority=10 so the compliance audit is written BEFORE downstream notification-composers read the row.

params.name: requires_platform_permission
params.description: Optional permission gate on the `platform_admin` guard. When set, the listener refuses to run if the triggering actor lacks the permission. Rarely used — impersonation listeners typically fire for compliance-driven side effects that must not skip.
